exampleJapanese: 春になると、歴史ある大学の中庭には新入生たちの期待と不安が入り混じった空気が漂い、桜の花びらが静かに石畳を覆っていく。
exampleEnglish: In spring, the courtyard of the historic university fills with an atmosphere of anticipation and anxiety among new students, while cherry blossom petals quietly cover the stone pavement.

exampleJapanese: 交渉は夜半まで続いたものの、最終的には双方が妥協案を受け入れ、長年停滞していた条約がようやく締結された。
exampleEnglish: Although the negotiations continued until the middle of the night, both sides ultimately accepted a compromise, and the treaty that had been stalled for years was finally concluded.

exampleJapanese: 彼は社内で一番若い役員でありながら、複雑な海外買収案件を冷静にまとめ上げ、その手腕を高く評価されている。
exampleEnglish: Despite being the youngest executive in the company, he calmly brought a complex overseas acquisition deal to completion and is highly regarded for his capabilities.

exampleJapanese: 研究に没頭するあまり、彼女は昨晩から何も食べておらず、同僚に指摘されるまで自分の疲労にすら気づいていなかった。
exampleEnglish: So absorbed in her research was she that she had not eaten anything since last night and did not even notice her own exhaustion until a colleague pointed it out.

exampleJapanese: 地方の小さなパン屋で偶然買った天然酵母のパンが驚くほど美味しく、その職人が何十年も同じ製法を守り続けている理由を理解した。
exampleEnglish: The naturally fermented bread I happened to buy at a small local bakery was astonishingly delicious, and I understood why the artisan had preserved the same method for decades.

exampleJapanese: 重要な商談の最中にコーヒーをこぼしてしまった彼は、隣にいた秘書から差し出された白いハンカチによって、かろうじて冷静さを保った。
exampleEnglish: After spilling coffee during an important business meeting, he barely managed to maintain his composure thanks to the white handkerchief offered by the secretary beside him.

exampleJapanese: 彼は利益の半分を地域医療の支援に寄付しているが、その行為を公の場で誇示したことは一度もない。
exampleEnglish: He donates half of his profits to support regional healthcare, yet he has never once boasted about it in public.

exampleJapanese: 事故の衝撃で左腕の感覚を一時的に失ったものの、彼女は長期のリハビリを経て再び外科医として現場に復帰した。
exampleEnglish: Although she temporarily lost sensation in her left arm due to the impact of the accident, she returned to work as a surgeon after undergoing long-term rehabilitation.

exampleJapanese: どれほど優れた技術を持っていても、人を信頼できなければ、大規模な研究プロジェクトを成功に導くことは難しい。
exampleEnglish: No matter how exceptional one’s technical skills may be, it is difficult to lead a large-scale research project to success without being able to trust people.

exampleJapanese: 新しい環境に慣れるまでには一月ほどかかったが、その間に彼は現地の文化や価値観を深く理解するようになった。
exampleEnglish: It took about one month for him to adjust to the new environment, but during that time he came to deeply understand the local culture and values.

exampleJapanese: その哲学者の講演には百人を超える聴衆が集まり、会場では予定時間を過ぎても活発な議論が続いていた。
exampleEnglish: More than one hundred people gathered for the philosopher’s lecture, and lively discussions continued at the venue even after the scheduled time had passed.

exampleJapanese: 彼は病気を理由に研究を中断せざるを得なかったが、その経験が後に医療倫理に関する重要な論文を書く契機となった。
exampleEnglish: He was forced to suspend his research because of illness, but that experience later became the catalyst for writing an important paper on medical ethics.

meanings: Film (Either protective film or photographic film)
exampleJapanese: デジタル技術が主流となった現在でも、彼女は独特の質感を求めて古いフィルムカメラを使い続けている。
exampleEnglish: Even now that digital technology has become mainstream, she continues to use an old film camera in pursuit of its distinctive texture.

exampleJapanese: 長時間のデスクワークで肩を痛めた彼は、医師の勧めで毎朝プールに通い、少しずつ体調を回復させている。
exampleEnglish: After injuring his shoulders from long hours of desk work, he began going to the pool every morning on his doctor’s recommendation and is gradually recovering his health.

exampleJapanese: 格式ある晩餐会では、料理ごとに異なるフォークを使い分ける作法を知らないだけで、教養の有無を判断されかねない。
exampleEnglish: At a formal banquet, simply not knowing the etiquette of using different forks for each course could lead others to question one’s level of refinement.

exampleJapanese: 栄養管理を徹底している彼でさえ、疲労が蓄積すると無性に香辛料の効いた豚肉料理を食べたくなるらしい。
exampleEnglish: Even he, who strictly manages his nutrition, apparently develops an intense craving for spicy pork dishes when fatigue builds up.

exampleJapanese: 長年対立していた二人の科学者は、気候変動に関する共同研究をきっかけに、次第に互いを尊重するようになった。
exampleEnglish: The two scientists, who had been in conflict for many years, gradually came to respect each other through their joint research on climate change.

exampleJapanese: 重要な証拠が発見されてからわずか二日で、警察は長年未解決だった事件の真相にたどり着いた。
exampleEnglish: Just two days after crucial evidence was discovered, the police arrived at the truth behind the case that had remained unsolved for years.

exampleJapanese: 冬の山村では、雪に閉ざされた静寂の中で、人々が古くから受け継がれてきた祭りの準備を黙々と進めていた。
exampleEnglish: In the mountain village during winter, amid the silence sealed in by snow, the people quietly carried on preparing for a festival that had been passed down for generations.

exampleJapanese: 古い法律を時代に合わせて改正しなければ、新しい技術によって生じる倫理的問題に対応することはできない。
exampleEnglish: If old laws are not revised to fit the times, it will be impossible to address the ethical problems created by new technologies.

exampleJapanese: 彼女は古文書の最後のページをめくった瞬間、それまで別々だと思われていた二つの事件が密接に関係していたことを悟った。
exampleEnglish: The moment she turned the final page of the ancient document, she realized that the two incidents previously thought to be unrelated were in fact closely connected.

exampleJapanese: 長時間の手術を終えた外科医は、病院の休憩室にある簡素なベッドに倒れ込むように横たわり、ようやく緊張から解放された。
exampleEnglish: After completing a long surgery, the surgeon collapsed onto the simple bed in the hospital break room and was finally released from the tension.

exampleJapanese: 高齢化が進む地域では、孤独を和らげる存在としてペットと暮らす高齢者が年々増えている。
exampleEnglish: In regions with advancing aging populations, the number of elderly people living with pets as companions to ease loneliness is increasing year by year.

exampleJapanese: 彼は祖父から譲り受けた古い万年筆を大切にしており、重要な契約書には必ずそのペンで署名すると決めている。
exampleEnglish: He treasures the old fountain pen inherited from his grandfather and has decided to sign all important contracts with that pen.

exampleJapanese: 国際法を勉強するうちに、彼女は単なる知識だけでなく、多様な価値観を理解する姿勢の重要性にも気づき始めた。
exampleEnglish: As she studied international law, she began to realize the importance not only of knowledge itself but also of having an attitude that seeks to understand diverse values.

exampleJapanese: 会議の最中、彼は無意識のうちにボールペンを回し続けており、その癖が周囲の集中を妨げていることに気づいていなかった。
exampleEnglish: During the meeting, he kept spinning his ballpoint pen unconsciously and did not realize that the habit was distracting those around him.

exampleJapanese: 彼はコートのポケットから古びた写真を取り出し、それが戦争で行方不明になった祖父の唯一の手がかりだと静かに語った。
exampleEnglish: He took an old photograph out of his coat pocket and quietly explained that it was the only clue to his grandfather, who had gone missing during the war.

exampleJapanese: 匿名の告発文が深夜に新聞社のポストへ投函されたことをきっかけに、大規模な汚職事件の調査が始まった。
exampleEnglish: The investigation into a large-scale corruption scandal began after an anonymous letter of accusation was dropped into the newspaper company’s mailbox late at night.

exampleJapanese: 緊張のあまり、彼は記者会見の直前までシャツのボタンを掛け違えていることに気づかなかった。
exampleEnglish: Because he was so nervous, he did not notice until just before the press conference that he had buttoned his shirt incorrectly.

exampleJapanese: 国際会議の期間中、そのホテルには各国の外交官や研究者が滞在しており、ロビーでは深夜まで議論が交わされていた。
exampleEnglish: During the international conference, diplomats and researchers from various countries were staying at the hotel, and discussions continued in the lobby late into the night.

exampleJapanese: 証拠隠滅を図った容疑者は、倉庫に火を放つために一本のマッチを擦ったが、その行動が監視カメラに記録されていた。
exampleEnglish: The suspect attempting to destroy evidence struck a single match to set the warehouse on fire, but the act was recorded by a security camera.

exampleJapanese: 深夜まで研究室に残っていた教授は、ふと窓の外に目をやると、無人のキャンパスを静かに覆う雪に気づいた。
exampleEnglish: The professor, who had remained in the laboratory until late at night, happened to glance out the window and noticed the snow quietly covering the empty campus.

exampleJapanese: 彼はわずか三十代で資産が一億円を超えたにもかかわらず、万が一会社が倒産した場合に備えて、質素な生活を崩さなかった。
exampleEnglish: Although his assets exceeded one hundred million yen while still in his thirties, he continued to live modestly in preparation for the unlikely event that his company might go bankrupt.

exampleJapanese: 討論会で右派の政治家が移民政策の厳格化を主張すると、会場の空気は一気に緊張感を帯びた。
exampleEnglish: When the right-wing politician argued for stricter immigration policies during the debate, the atmosphere in the venue immediately grew tense.

exampleJapanese: 長期の干ばつによって水の供給が不安定になったため、政府は工業地帯への使用制限を検討し始めた。
exampleEnglish: Because the prolonged drought made the water supply unstable, the government began considering usage restrictions for industrial zones.

exampleJapanese: 観光客向けに派手な装飾を施した店よりも、地元の人々が通い続ける古い食堂のほうが、はるかに本格的な味を提供していた。
exampleEnglish: Rather than the shops decorated flamboyantly for tourists, the old diner frequented by locals offered a far more authentic taste.

exampleJapanese: 重要な契約交渉を目前に控えていたにもかかわらず、彼は三日も連絡を絶って姿を消したため、社内は大混乱に陥った。
exampleEnglish: Despite the crucial contract negotiations being just around the corner, he cut off all contact and disappeared for three whole days, throwing the company into chaos.

exampleJapanese: 医師として数十年働いてきた彼女でさえ、戦地から運び込まれた負傷者たちの惨状を見るたびに、言葉を失った。
exampleEnglish: Even she, despite having worked as a doctor for decades, was left speechless each time she saw the horrific condition of the wounded brought in from the battlefield.

exampleJapanese: 新しい校長は、生徒だけでなく教師や保護者も含めてみんなが安心して意見を言える環境を作ろうとしていた。
exampleEnglish: The new principal was trying to create an environment where everyone—not only students, but also teachers and parents—could express their opinions without fear.

exampleJapanese: 台風の影響で物流網が寸断され、離島には六日間も食料が届かない状態が続いていた。
exampleEnglish: Due to the typhoon disrupting the logistics network, the remote island went without food deliveries for as long as six days.

exampleJapanese: 長年にわたって不正会計を見逃してきた幹部たちは、内部告発によってようやく世間の厳しい目にさらされることになった。
exampleEnglish: The executives who had overlooked fraudulent accounting for many years were finally exposed to the harsh scrutiny of the public through a whistleblower’s revelations.

exampleJapanese: 地震発生後、沿岸部では高さ十メートルを超える津波が観測され、住民に即時避難命令が出された。
exampleEnglish: After the earthquake, a tsunami exceeding ten meters in height was observed along the coast, and residents were ordered to evacuate immediately.

exampleJapanese: 交渉はすでに決裂しており、双方ともにもう譲歩する余地は残されていなかった。
exampleEnglish: The negotiations had already broken down, and neither side had any room left to compromise.

exampleJapanese: 研究チームは、感染拡大を防ぐにはもっと迅速な情報共有体制が必要だと政府に訴えた。
exampleEnglish: The research team appealed to the government, arguing that a much faster information-sharing system was necessary to prevent the spread of infections.

exampleJapanese: 考古学者たちは、沈没船から引き揚げられた青銅製の遺物が、交易ルートの解明に重要な手がかりを与えると考えている。
exampleEnglish: Archaeologists believe that the bronze artifacts recovered from the sunken ship will provide important clues for uncovering ancient trade routes.

exampleJapanese: 長年閉ざされていた大学の門が地域住民にも開放されることになり、文化交流への期待が高まっている。
exampleEnglish: The university gates, which had been closed for many years, are now set to be opened to local residents as well, raising expectations for cultural exchange.

exampleJapanese: 環境問題を単なる経済成長の障害として扱う限り、持続可能な社会の実現は難しいだろう。
exampleEnglish: As long as environmental issues are treated merely as obstacles to economic growth, achieving a sustainable society will remain difficult.

exampleJapanese: 長期間休みを取らずに働き続けた結果、彼は慢性的な疲労と不眠に悩まされるようになった。
exampleEnglish: As a result of continuing to work without taking a break for a long period, he began to suffer from chronic fatigue and insomnia.

exampleJapanese: 医師からしばらく休むよう勧められていたにもかかわらず、彼女は重要な裁判を理由に仕事を続けた。
exampleEnglish: Although her doctor had advised her to take some time off, she continued working because of an important trial.

exampleJapanese: 遭難した登山者たちは、吹雪に閉ざされた山小屋の中で救助隊の到着を待ち続けていた。
exampleEnglish: The stranded climbers continued waiting for the rescue team’s arrival inside a mountain hut sealed off by the blizzard.

exampleJapanese: 周囲に無謀だと言われても、彼は誰も成功できないと言われていた新事業をやる決意を変えなかった。
exampleEnglish: Even though those around him called it reckless, he did not change his determination to carry out the new business venture that everyone said was impossible to succeed in.

exampleJapanese: その有名な経済学者は、格差の拡大が民主主義そのものを不安定にすると警告していた。
exampleEnglish: The famous economist warned that widening inequality would destabilize democracy itself.

exampleJapanese: 感染症の拡大を受けて、研究施設は八日間にわたり完全に閉鎖され、すべての実験が中断された。
exampleEnglish: In response to the spread of the infectious disease, the research facility was completely shut down for eight days, and all experiments were suspended.

exampleJapanese: 容疑者は四日間にわたる厳しい取り調べの末、ついに事件への関与を認めた。
exampleEnglish: After four days of intense interrogation, the suspect finally admitted involvement in the case.

exampleJapanese: 契約書を十分に読まずに署名した結果、彼は不利な条件を受け入れざるを得なくなった。
exampleEnglish: As a result of signing the contract without reading it carefully, he was forced to accept unfavorable terms.

exampleJapanese: 夜が更けるにつれて、記者たちは選挙結果を待ちながら緊張した面持ちで速報を更新し続けていた。
exampleEnglish: As the night grew later, the reporters continued updating breaking news reports with tense expressions while awaiting the election results.

exampleJapanese: 来月から施行される新しい労働法によって、長時間労働に対する企業の責任がこれまで以上に厳しく問われることになる。
exampleEnglish: Under the new labor law taking effect next month, companies will be held more strictly accountable than ever for excessive working hours.

exampleJapanese: 来週予定されている首脳会談では、エネルギー安全保障と貿易摩擦が主要な議題になると見られている。
exampleEnglish: At the summit meeting scheduled for next week, energy security and trade friction are expected to be the main topics of discussion.

exampleJapanese: 来年には自動運転技術に関する国際的な規制基準が導入される見通しで、多くの企業が対応を迫られている。
exampleEnglish: International regulatory standards concerning autonomous driving technology are expected to be introduced next year, forcing many companies to adapt.

exampleJapanese: 災害によって通信網が遮断された地域では、古いラジオだけが外部情報を得るための唯一の手段となっていた。
exampleEnglish: In areas where communication networks were cut off by the disaster, old radios became the only means of obtaining outside information.

exampleJapanese: 祖父の書斎から見つかった古いラジオカセットには、若い頃の家族の会話がそのまま録音されていた。
exampleEnglish: On the old radio cassette player found in my grandfather’s study, conversations from the family’s younger days had been recorded just as they were.

exampleJapanese: デジタル配信が主流となった現在でも、温かみのある音を求めてレコードを収集する若者は少なくない。
exampleEnglish: Even now that digital distribution has become the norm, quite a few young people still collect records in search of their warm sound.

exampleJapanese: その高級レストランは、政治家や実業家が非公式な会談を行う場所として長年利用されてきた。
exampleEnglish: That high-end restaurant has long been used as a place where politicians and business executives hold unofficial meetings.

exampleJapanese: 重要な商談を控えた彼は、しわ一つない白いワイシャツに袖を通し、静かに気持ちを引き締めた。
exampleEnglish: With an important business negotiation ahead of him, he slipped into a perfectly wrinkle-free white dress shirt and quietly steeled himself.

exampleJapanese: 長年同じ研究に携わってきた彼には、わずかな数値の変化を見るだけで実験の異常が分かるようになっていた。
exampleEnglish: Having worked on the same research for many years, he had become able to recognize abnormalities in the experiment just by seeing slight changes in the numerical data.

exampleJapanese: 私が記者として最も衝撃を受けたのは、紛争地域で暮らす子どもたちが、日常のように爆撃の音を受け入れていたことだった。
exampleEnglish: What shocked me most as a journalist was that children living in conflict zones had come to accept the sound of bombings as part of everyday life.

meanings: To cross (Usually used when moving from one side to another especially across roads, bridges, rivers, etc)
exampleJapanese: 警察の追跡を逃れるため、容疑者たちは国境を渡る直前に偽造パスポートを処分した。
exampleEnglish: To evade police pursuit, the suspects disposed of their forged passports just before crossing the border.
